findings.title: Arbitrary V3-style pools can forge callbacks and steal approved user funds

best_agent_match.title: Arbitrary V3-style pools can forge callbacks and steal approved user funds

findings.title: Public routes can sweep router-held ETH, ERC20, and Bento balances because input accounting ignores contract-owned inventory

best_agent_match.title: Public routes can sweep router-held ERC20 and Bento balances because input accounting ignores contract-owned inventory

findings.title: Native unwrap pays out the router's entire ETH balance instead of only the requested amount

best_agent_match.title: Native unwrap pays out the router's entire ETH balance instead of only the requested amount

findings.title: `processUserERC20` is not bound to the declared `tokenIn`, allowing malicious routes to pull arbitrary approved assets from the caller

best_agent_match.title: Zero-amount Bento deposits let arbitrary callers capture surplus tokens parked at BentoBox

rejected_candidates.title: Zero-amount Bento deposits let arbitrary callers capture surplus tokens parked at BentoBox
rejected_candidates.reason: This appears to rely on BentoBox's own public skim/deposit-from-vault behavior (`deposit(..., from = address(bentoBox))`), so the router is not introducing a new privilege or theft path.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing slippage protection in swapUniV2
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract enforces a route-level `amountOutMin` at the end of `processRouteInternal`; slippage is not unchecked just because individual hops lack per-hop minimums.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection for Trident pool swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: The final `amountOutMin` check applies to Trident routes as well, so this is not a separate missing-slippage bug.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline/timestamp validation for route execution
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing deadlines are a user-experience/control limitation, not a standalone protocol exploit when the caller already chooses whether to submit the transaction and can set `amountOutMin`.

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted arbitrary recipient for BentoBox deposits
rejected_candidates.reason: Route-controlled recipients are intended behavior by themselves. The reportable issue is the missing binding between external `tokenIn` and the route-selected source asset, which is captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing reentrancy protection in swap callbacks
rejected_candidates.reason: The actionable callback flaw is the arbitrary-pool/authentication issue already kept. No additional exploitable reentrant path is demonstrated beyond that.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited token approvals for external tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect premise: this contract does not grant token approvals in the cited code paths; it only uses transfers and transferFrom.

rejected_candidates.title: Division truncation leads to precision loss in share distribution
rejected_candidates.reason: This is bounded rounding dust from integer math and the share-based route format, not realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing validation of swapData for Trident pools
rejected_candidates.reason: `swapData` is intentionally pool-specific opaque calldata. Without an independent authorization failure, arbitrary data alone is not a router vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient input validation on 'to' address
rejected_candidates.reason: A caller choosing a bad recipient is self-inflicted misuse, not a protocol bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Manipulable balance check using msg.sender
rejected_candidates.reason: The stated callback-based manipulation is speculative. The concrete balance-check failure is the internal-inventory and asset-mismatch issues already captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Slot undrain protection uses unchecked subtraction incorrectly
rejected_candidates.reason: Leaving dust when balance is 1 is an edge effect of the deliberate undrain pattern, not an exploitable vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Silent failure possible in transferValueAndprocessRoute
rejected_candidates.reason: This concerns revert-message quality only and does not create realistic loss, theft, lockup, or DoS.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation that tokenOut is different from tokenIn
rejected_candidates.reason: Swapping a token into itself is a route-construction mistake, not an unauthorized or protocol-level exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: No event emissions for critical operations
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of events is an observability issue, not a security vulnerability.
